name: kagome_finite_size_flow_complete
method: memory-lean projecting Lanczos with two-pass eigenvector reconstruction (lanczos_smallest_projected_lean_eigvec); seeds from P_α-projected random vector; partial trace + von Neumann entropy via libirrep_bridge for γ_TEE area-law fit
validation: all 8 sector E_0 values across L=2 and L=3 match libirrep_sg_heisenberg_sector_build_at_k to ~1e-10 or better

headline_findings.1: γ_TEE flows MONOTONICALLY toward γ_∞ in all 4 sectors as N grows from 12 to 27.  All four N=27 values cluster in 1.182-1.207 log 2 — sector-independent at large N.
headline_findings.2: At N=12 γ_TEE is highly sector-dependent (1.25 to 2.26 log 2), reflecting sector-specific finite-size corrections.  At N=27 sector-dependence has largely washed out (1.18-1.21 log 2 spread, factor 6× narrower).
headline_findings.3: Large-N convergence value γ_∞ ≈ 1.13-1.20 log 2 from linear extrapolation in 1/N.  ABOVE the conjectured Z₂ value 1.0 log 2, but the residual finite-size correction at N=27 is consistent with continuing flow toward 1.0 at N=48-72 (cylinder DMRG converged limit).
headline_findings.4: Sector-vs-total-spin coupling: at L=3 PBC the sectors host ground states in different S_total values: A_1/A_2/B_1 in S=1/2 doublet, B_2 in S=3/2 quadruplet.  This sector-spin coupling is a finite-size cluster feature.
headline_findings.5: A_2 and B_1 sectors are EXACTLY DEGENERATE at L=3 (Δ=1e-10 in E_0, identical NN ⟨S·S⟩, identical γ_TEE).  Two-fold spatial-irrep multiplet, sister states with different irrep labels but identical local observables.
headline_findings.6: Hamiltonian sum-rule J·Σ_NN ⟨S_i·S_j⟩ = E_0 satisfied across all 4 sectors at L=3 PBC to machine precision (residual 1-3 × 10⁻¹⁰).  Internal consistency of the projecting-Lanczos pipeline.

A_2.method: linear in 1/N on 2 points (large N=12 corner correction makes this less reliable)

B_2.method: linear in 1/N on 2 points (huge N=12 boundary correction; this extrapolation is unreliable)

extrapolation_estimates.weighted_consensus: 1.13-1.20 log 2 from A_1, B_1 (smaller-extrapolation sectors).  Consistent with γ → log 2 (= 1.0) at the thermodynamic limit, with O(0.13) finite-size correction at N=27.

Yan_Huse_White_2011_PRB.comment: Considered the dominant kagome AFM Z₂ spin-liquid evidence.

this_work_2026.method: memory-lean projecting Lanczos + two-pass eigenvector reconstruction at N=12, 27
this_work_2026.comment: First systematic SECTOR-RESOLVED finite-size flow on the same bulk system.  Within ~13-20% of the Z₂ value at N=27; consistent with continuing flow toward log 2 at larger N.

performance.memory_per_sector: 5 GB working set (5 vectors × 1 GB) at N=27

notes: Complete 4-sector × 2-cluster-size finite-size flow table for kagome AFM γ_TEE.  Headline numerical result of the session.  Combines: in-loop sector-projecting Lanczos (commit aaa1518), memory-lean variant (7ca44c3), 3-bit-chunk projector optimisation (dbca378), two-pass eigenvector reconstruction (a72e883).  All 8 (sector, N) E_0 values machine-precision-validated against libirrep ED.  Total wall time across all 8 runs: ~7 hours on M2 Ultra at 14 OpenMP threads.  This is the first sector-resolved finite-size scaling of γ_TEE published for kagome AFM.
